ADCO split adoption retry worker 019f3051-957a-76f1-8cd1-658620da147c is the screenshot case: it is bound to temporary worktree f7b3, reported that the isolated worktree was missing, self-created it from main HEAD, and produced a 3-file diff. The thread was archived and the diff is rejected evidence; missing-cwd worker self-recreation is now hard-blocked by missing-cwd-worker-self-recreated-invalid.

ADCO split adoption fork rescue worker 019f3058-9213-73b3-9c60-a6284b6b77e9 produced a valid receipt and the bounded gate fixture was adopted as 5101dbf, but this is a partial ThreadOps validation hardening, not a claim that all legacy dirty worktree material has been ported.

Archived temporary ADCO worker worktrees such as d6f2, 0e1f, and f436 can show 'current working directory missing' when reopened in Codex; f7b3 currently exists but is dirty rejected evidence. The ADCO main path /Users/jinjungao/work/ad-creative-orchestrator and retained legacy dirty evidence worktree remain verified separately.

ADCO legacy dirty disposition reviewer 019f3065-dd2a-7df0-beaf-8f9fbc780742 concluded adopt_now=none: the legacy branch has no commits ahead of main, main is ahead by 3 commits, and the remaining dirty diff is evidence-only keep_open rather than safe migration material.

Goal-readiness reviewer 019f306b-5652-7322-9ac2-ecdd651fae2f did not produce THREE_PROJECT_GOAL_READINESS_AUDIT_RECEIPT; it booted COS/no_dispatch despite being a role-specific worker, so it is rejected evidence and was archived.

Bounded rescue reviewer 019f3075-a3e9-7660-9813-dc39a8cb0d04 was dispatched after adding COS_WORKER_BYPASS and did not emit COS_BOOT_RECEIPT. It was initially interrupted by coordinator error while active, then unarchived and resumed; it produced THREE_PROJECT_GOAL_READINESS_AUDIT_RECEIPT with complete_ready=false.

Skill-maintainer worker 019f3084-0e9b-7900-8724-6db0121cf919 added heartbeat run receipt hardening, but its self-reported thread_id was unknown; adoption used controller readback for the real thread id and main-worktree validation.

Temporary heartbeat run receipt smoke target 019f3090-c370-7952-91a9-ce3ca910e4ee fired twice but emitted target_thread_verified=false and used root thread 019f2354-f00c-7132-90d7-fb6c26ff2ecf instead of the actual target; it is rejected as progress evidence and now covered by heartbeat-run-target-unverified-invalid.

Natural heartbeat acceptance reviewer 019f30b4-34b3-79c1-9d8c-3b44d90571d0 emitted COS_NATURAL_HEARTBEAT_ACCEPTANCE_REVIEW_RECEIPT with verdict FAIL before the configured next due time; it is archived and rejected as acceptance evidence, and now covered by natural-heartbeat-before-due-fail-invalid due-window validation.

Natural heartbeat and release-gap reviewer 019f30d1-5af7-7862-8e71-af63ca2765c0 produced a valid receipt with complete_ready=false; it confirms the current blocker is waiting for the natural due window rather than a newly failing gate.
After archival, the failed temporary worktree paths d6f2 and 0e1f were no longer present; no diff from those failed paths is claimed or adopted.
Legacy dirty worktree /Users/jinjungao/.codex/worktrees/adco-skill-hardening/ad-creative-orchestrator still exists and remains the only retained dirty-worktree evidence.

The heartbeat smoke proves explicit Skill invocation can fire via Codex heartbeat and produce COS_BOOT_RECEIPT, but as of 2026-07-06T01:54:49+08:00 the long-running six-hour cos automation is not due until 2026-07-06T07:43:28.193+08:00, so natural-fire completion remains unproven rather than failed.
DIR_LIVE_ACCEPTANCE_GAP_RECEIPT now confirms the DIR project remains NEEDS_USER for real live acceptance despite passing local validation.

ADCO_LOCAL_FRESH_CLONE_SMOKE_RECEIPT now confirms local fresh-clone and installed-copy smoke pass for local HEAD fa60638; remote CI is still not checked because origin/main remains 48f2193 and no push was performed.
evidence_limits: This round proves current gates/tests, local worktree evidence, target-thread readback, and a bounded heartbeat smoke in an idle target thread; it does not prove every future Codex surface or an in-progress target thread will accept a heartbeat mid-turn.

COS_WORKER_BYPASS is now encoded in the Skill, AGENTS routing snippet, role prompts, and activation fixtures. The live rescue reviewer verified the direction by not emitting COS_BOOT_RECEIPT and later returning a readiness receipt, but the receipt still blocks goal completion on natural heartbeat, ADCO legacy dirty evidence, and missing domain receipts.

HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT/COS_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT hardens future heartbeat runs, but the current six-hour cos natural fire is still not proven until the next due window is read back from the target thread.

The new heartbeat run receipt smoke proves the stronger receipt marker can be emitted, but it also proved unverified target readback can be misreported by the model. Release evidence therefore requires validator-enforced target_thread_verified=true and target metadata readback, not self-report alone.

The strict positive smoke used an explicit target id/title/cwd in the automation prompt, so it verifies the prompt shape and receipt schema rather than independently proving the model can discover target metadata without being given it.
create_thread returned a handler error while duplicate threads materialized; duplicate 019f3036-fe20-7a00-b5eb-14ea205bad24 was archived/interrupted and rejected as evidence.

Transient invalid placeholder THREAD_DISPATCH_RECEIPT rows with thread_id=pending or dispatch_pending were emitted during continuations and are explicitly rejected as evidence; only rows with a real thread_id or a non-empty pending_worktree_id are valid dispatch evidence.
Project-main COS threads remain open by design; only completed worker/review threads should be archived.
Human-readable dispatch summary hardening is validated locally and installed-copy synced; it has not yet been observed in a future organic ADCO/DIR worker dispatch after this patch.
